inputs.titre: Jeanne d'Évreux Becomes Queen of France

reason: The image depicts what appears to be Jeanne d'Évreux being crowned Queen of France. The clothing, architecture, and lighting evoke the 14th century. While the general aesthetic aligns with the historical period, several minor details are questionable and could be anachronistic. The materials and cut of some of the clothing appear slightly more modern than medieval, and the hairstyles might be too contemporary. The stained glass, while stylistically appropriate, could contain inaccuracies in its design. The lighting is also very bright. Overall, the image gets many things right, but requires some attention to detail to be historically perfect. The crown also looks rather plain, more of a circlet than a bejeweled crown.
